tooltips.name: The name of the scenario.
tooltips.startDate: The start DTG of the scenario in UTC (YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS.sss).
tooltips.endDate: The end DTG of the scenario in UTC (YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS.sss).

core.beforeStartTime: Simulation time is before the scenario start time. Pausing at start time.
core.afterStopTime: Simulation time is after the scenario stop time. Pausing at stop time.

help.overview: Scenario Management gives your session a named context: a scenario with a name, a description, and an optional analysis window with a start and end time. The simulation clock is then kept inside that window, and the whole definition can be saved to a file and reloaded later or shared with a teammate.
help.imgAlt: The Scenario Settings menu with name, description, and time window fields
help.imgCaption: Define a scenario name, description, and an optional start and end time.
help.window: When you set a Scenario Start or Scenario End (UTC, formatted YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS.sss), the simulation clock is clamped to that window: if time drifts before the start or past the end, the clock pauses at the boundary and a notification explains why. Leave the fields empty for an unconstrained scenario.
help.howToUse:
1. Open Scenario Management from the bottom menu (Tools mode).
  2. Enter a Scenario Name (required) and an optional Description.
  3. Optionally set the Scenario Start and Scenario End times in UTC.
  4. Click Update Scenario to apply. The time window is enforced immediately.
  5. Use Save Scenario to download the definition as a compressed .kts file, and Load Scenario to restore one from disk.
help.tip1: If playback seems stuck, check whether the clock is pinned at a scenario boundary; clear the start and end fields to release it.
help.tip2: Scenario files are small compressed .kts files, handy for sharing a common analysis window with a team.
help.tip3: Date fields validate as you type; an invalid date is highlighted and rejected when updating.
